mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, equity discount rates compress and forward equity valuations expand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.91% suggests limited room for further cuts
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 149,279 thousand indicates near-trend employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Payroll growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of months, or correlation with forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month, consumer demand weakness signals deteriorating corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices earnings forecasts downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 509,025 million reflects recent consumer activity
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or the signal exhibits zero correlation with forward returns over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment rises above 4.0%, signaling simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.23 and UNRATE at 3.9% show tight supply-labor conditions
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Joint trigger condition produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized while retail sales growth remains positive, indicating broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations through multiplier effects on construction, materials, and consumer durables over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200 thousand AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,168 thousand is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint condition produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 62% of triggered months, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.15) when tested over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates turn negative and equity valuations compress as the Fed is perceived as behind the inflation curve, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 251.85 and FEDFUNDS at 1.91% suggest potential real rate compression
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Trigger condition produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the real rate spread variable shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.9% is near historical lows, limiting upside risk
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or the signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts contract below 1,100 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand weakness across the economy that triggers equity repricing downward over 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are cut.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND HOUST < 1100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18,514.6 billion and HOUST at 1,168 thousand show mixed demand signals
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across growth and housing
failure_condition: Joint condition produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term p-value exceeds 0.20 in logistic regression over rolling 2-year windows.
